name: example.fail_fast_demo
description: On example.com, take a baseline screenshot, then click a corner that triggers nothing. The runner should byte-compare the post-click capture with the baseline, see they're identical, and fail-fast WITHOUT spending a classifier round-trip — because if the screen hasn't moved the answer can only be 'still on example.com'.

steps.note: baseline: just establish 'lastEvidence' so step 2 has something to diff against

Click top-left corner — example.com has nothing there. Expecting 'page_after_login' (a screen that doesn't exist) is a guaranteed-fail. With byte-equal detection it fails in milliseconds instead of after a 3-7s Qwen call.
